zh.lifeSummary: 當Elizabeth FitzWilliam於1426年出生在英國英格蘭林可爾史爾馬波索普時，她的父親Thomas FitzWilliam IV38歲，母親Margaret Ann Dymoke21歲。她於1457年與Robert Eyre II結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和2個女兒的父母。她去世於1535年在英國德比夏爾貝克韋爾_享年109歲。

nameMeanings.name: FitzWilliam
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English, Scottish, and Irish (of Norman origin): from Anglo-Norman French fiz ‘son’ (see Fitz ) + the personal name William .

variants.name: Fitz
meanings: English: from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (from Latin filius), used originally to distinguish a son from a father bearing the same personal name and then conventionalized as a hereditary surname, or short for a surname of which fiz ‘son’ was the first element, for example Fitzhenry and Fitzroy .
German: from a vernacular pet form of the personal name Vinzenz (see Vincent ) or Vitus (see Vito , compare Veit ). Compare also Fietz .

nameMeanings.name: Elizabeth
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: The usual spelling of   Elisabeth   in English. It is recorded in the medieval period, but was made popular by being borne by  Queen Elizabeth I  of England (  1533–1603 ). In the 20th century it again became extremely fashionable, partly because it was the name of  Elizabeth Bowes-Lyon  (  1900–2002 ), who in  1936  became Queen Elizabeth as the wife of King George VI, and after his death in  1952  achieved great public affection as Queen Mother for nearly half a century. Even more influentially, it is the name of her daughter  Queen Elizabeth II  (  b. 1926 ).

variants.name: Elisabeth
meanings: The spelling of Elizabeth used in the Authorized Version of the New Testament, and in most modern European languages. This was the name of the mother of John the Baptist (Luke 1:60). Etymologically, the name means ‘God is my oath’, and is therefore identical with Elisheba, the name of the wife of Aaron according to the genealogy at Exodus 6:23. The final element seems to have been altered by association with Hebrew shabbāth ‘Sabbath’.

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: When Elizabeth FitzWilliam was born in 1426, in Mablethorpe, Lincolnshire, England, United Kingdom, her father, Thomas FitzWilliam IV, was 38 and her mother, Margaret Ann Dymoke, was 21. She married Robert Eyre II in 1457. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 2 daughters. She died in 1535, in Bakewell, Derbyshire, England, at the age of 109.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Elizabeth FitzWilliam est née en 1426, à Mablethorpe, Lincolnshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Thomas FitzWilliam IV, avait 38 ans et sa mère, Margaret Ann Dymoke, avait 21 ans. Elle a épousé Robert Eyre II en 1457. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 2 filles. Elle est décédée en 1535, à Bakewell, Derbyshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 109 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Elizabeth FitzWilliam 1426 in Mablethorpe, Lincolnshire, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Thomas FitzWilliam IV 38 und ihre Mutter Margaret Ann Dymoke 21. ko.lifeSummary: Elizabeth FitzWilliam(이)가 1426년 영국 잉글랜드 링코실르 마블레소에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Thomas FitzWilliam IV(은)는 38세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Margaret Ann Dymoke(은)는 21세였다. 그녀는 1457년 Robert Eyre II(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1535년 영국 더비실르 베이케웰에서 109세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Elizabeth FitzWilliam родилась в 1426 г. в местности маблетаурп, линколншир, Англия, Великобритания, её отцу по имени Thomas FitzWilliam IV было 38 лет, а матери, которую звали Margaret Ann Dymoke, 21. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Robert Eyre II в 1457 г. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 2 дочери. Она умерла в 1535 г. в местности беикюелл, дирбишир, Англия в возрасте 109 лет.
